[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.hashMarket] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a valid offer exists on a market with MAX_COLLATERALS (128) collateral params and the maker has authorized the EcrecoverRatifier, call take() with a ratifierData encoding a depth-20 Merkle proof, forcing isRatified() to execute HashLib.hashOffer() -> HashLib.hashMarket() looping 128 times plus HashLib.isLeaf() looping 20 times in a single take(), violating the invariant that a single take() call must not consume disproportionate gas relative to its economic value, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS of the take() entry point by exhausting block gas when composed in multicall()?
